label: Model servers & inference runtimes

node.label: Model servers & inference runtimes
node.definition: Model servers expose trained models through batching, scheduling, caching, versioning, routing, and runtime-specific execution interfaces.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Model servers & inference runtimes is to connect Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion, Object storage, sharding & checkpoints to downstream capabilities including Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, Speculative, parallel & assisted decoding, Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference.
examples: substrate for Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling
substrate for Speculative, parallel & assisted decoding
substrate for Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion, Object storage, sharding & checkpoints.

dependencies.rationale: Model servers & inference runtimes turns Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Model servers & inference runtimes turns Object storage, sharding & checkpoints into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Model servers & inference runtimes turns Transformers into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
